title: Beginner: Intro to Machine Learning
description: Build strong fundamentals in datasets, supervised learning, and model evaluation.

sections.title: What is Machine Learning?
sections.intro: Machine learning is a branch of AI where systems learn patterns from data instead of following only hand-written rules.
sections.example: Spam filtering models learn from labeled emails (spam/not spam) and predict labels for new emails.
sections.exercise: List three daily apps that use prediction (for example: maps, recommendations, spam filters).

sections.title: Training Data and Labels
sections.intro: In supervised learning, each example has input features and a known output label used to teach a model.
sections.example: House-price data includes area, location, and age as features, and final sale price as the label.
sections.exercise: For a movie-rating predictor, identify possible features and the target label.

sections.title: Evaluation Basics
sections.intro: A model must be tested on unseen data. Metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 help evaluate quality.
sections.example: In medical screening, recall is often prioritized so fewer true cases are missed.
sections.exercise: Write one scenario where high precision matters more than high recall.

references: Adapted from public educational summaries in Wikipedia (Machine learning, Supervised learning).
Adapted from openly accessible tutorials and documentation-style ML introductions.
